title: Machine-guided design of cell-type-targeting cis-regulatory elements
methods: zebrafish and mouse transgenic reporter assays
data_sources: ENCODE DHS peaks and H3K27ac ChIP-seq
core_conclusion: A machine-learning platform (CODA) powered by a deep CNN (Malinois) trained on 798,064 MPRA-measured sequences can design synthetic cis-regulatory elements that surpass all naturally occurring genomic sequences in driving cell-type-specific gene expression across three human cell lines (94.1% specificity vs. 40.6% for DHS-natural sequences). Synthetic CREs achieve specificity through unique combinations of on-target activating and off-target repressing TF motifs rarely co-deployed in the human genome, and retain tissue-level specificity in zebrafish and mouse in vivo.
why_it_matters: Unlocks programmable cell-type-targeted gene therapy without depending on naturally evolved elements; demonstrates that synthetic regulatory sequences outperform natural ones at scale; closes the model-to-experiment loop by validating 77,157 machine-designed sequences; reveals interpretable TF-grammar programs driving specificity; confirms cross-species in vivo generalization.
why_nature: Landmark proof-of-concept at genome scale (77,157 sequences tested); generalizable CODA platform applicable to any CRE design objective; direct therapeutic relevance (CRISPR, gene replacement, targeted delivery); cross-disciplinary integration of deep learning, genomics, synthetic biology, and developmental biology; open data and reproducible tools.
added_value_to_my_research: Conditional generative models for CRE design -> same paradigm for generating synthetic spatial patterns or hypothetical urban configurations for accessibility scenario planning
SIG + NMF explainability stack -> transferable recipe for spatially coherent feature-attribution programs in MGWR/explainable spatial ML
Genomic foundation model fine-tuning for tissue-specific prediction -> direct analog to fine-tuning geospatial foundation models on local health-facility utilization data
Uncertainty-aware nomination (ensemble/Bayesian) -> integrating epistemic uncertainty into 2SFCA/R2SFCA accessibility estimates for policy-robust resource allocation
GNN-based motif-syntax modelling -> physics-informed graph learning with structural priors, analogous to encoding geographic contiguity in spatial GNNs

related.title: Semantic design of functional de novo genes from a genomic language model
related.relation: conditional generative model for de novo DNA sequence design, extending CODA's iterative optimization to single-pass generation

related.title: Multiomics and deep learning dissect regulatory syntax in human development
related.relation: multi-omics deep learning for regulatory syntax in primary tissues, extending Malinois beyond transformed cell lines

related.title: A foundation model of transcription across human cell types
related.relation: foundation model of transcription that could replace Enformer as the in silico tissue-specificity bridge in CODA

related.title: Evaluating deep learning for predicting epigenomic profiles
related.relation: rigorous benchmarking of genomic deep-learning failures, motivating uncertainty quantification in Malinois

related.title: A software framework for end-to-end genomic sequence analysis with deep learning
related.relation: software backbone for graph-augmented sequence models extending CODA's motif-syntax analysis

related.title: Regulatory grammar in human promoters uncovered by MPRA-based deep learning
related.relation: MPRA-based deep learning to decode regulatory grammar in promoters, directly complementary to the CRE enhancer focus of this paper

figures.caption: Malinois deep CNN accurately predicts cell-type-specific CRE activity from sequence alone (Pearson r = 0.88–0.89 on held-out chromosomes 7 and 13)
CODA's iterative design loop and empirical MPRA validation showing synthetic CREs achieve 94.1% specificity vs. 40.6% for DHS-natural sequences
Motif contribution scores, co-occurrence patterns, and NMF program decomposition revealing the TF grammar of cell-type specificity
In vivo validation: liver-specific CREs in zebrafish (27/36 animals) and neuron-specific synN1 restricted to neocortical layer 6 in postnatal mice
